items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` **Service directory:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` **Triggered by:** PR #57907 by `@jsquire` ## What Changed PR #57907 retired the `sharelink` stand-alone sample (deprecated KV-managed storage accounts feature) and updated stale links in `MigrationGuide.md`. However, the Table of Contents entry for "Managing shared access signatures" was left in place despite the corresponding section body being absent from the document. ## Gaps Found - `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` contains a Table of Contents link `[Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures)` (line 19) that points to a section that does not exist in the document body. This creates a broken anchor link and misleads readers into expecting documentation for a deprecated feature. - No mention anywhere in the guide that the KV-managed storage accounts / SAS feature has been deprecated, which could confuse users migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault` who used that feature.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/MigrationGuide.md` **Action:** Edit Two changes are needed in this file: **Change 1 — Remove the orphaned ToC entry** (around line 19): Remove this line from the Table of Contents: ```markdown - [Managing shared access signatures](#managing-shared-access-signatures) ``` **Change 2 — Add a deprecation note for managed storage / SAS** after the "Deleting secrets" section and before the "Additional samples" section. Insert the following new section: ```markdown ### Managing shared access signatures The Azure Key Vault managed storage accounts feature — including SAS definition management — has been [deprecated]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Microsoft recommends storing SAS tokens or storage account keys directly as Key Vault secrets instead of using the managed storage accounts feature. If you were previously using `KeyVaultClient` methods such as `SetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionAsync`, `GetSasDefinitionsAsync`, or `RegenerateStorageAccountKeyAsync` from `Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault`, you should migrate to managing your storage credentials manually and storing them as ordinary Key Vault secrets via `SecretClient.SetSecretAsync`. For more information, see [Manage storage account keys with Key Vault and the Azure CLI (legacy)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet infrastructure is needed for this change — the new section is prose-only with no embedded code samples. ### Step 3: Verify README structure No README changes are required; this is a MigrationGuide-only fix. Verify `MigrationGuide.md` sections remain in correct order: 1. Migration benefits ✅ 2. General changes ✅ - Package and namespaces - Separate clients - Client constructors - Setting secrets - Getting secrets - Listing secrets - Listing secret versions - Deleting secrets - **Managing shared access signatures** ← new prose section to add 3. Additional samples ✅ 4. Support ✅ ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 keyvault` — confirm no README snippet regressions 4. `dotnet format sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [KeyVault] Docs: Remove orphaned ToC entry in Secrets MigrationGuide after sharelink retirement
